title: aster quadratus
intro: Aster quadratus, also known as the white aster or four-leaf aster, is a perennial wildflower that belongs to the daisy family. It is native to the eastern United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Aster quadratus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support respiratory health, relieve colds and flu, and soothe digestive issues.
